V-M-022.checks: User-facing device APIs expose only the caller's devices, allow deterministic create or revoke or rotate flows, keep one peer per logical device, and Phase-28 cannot provision VPN resources before email verification.
V-M-022.commands: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_verified_registration.py tests/test_device_api.py tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py -v
V-M-022.anchors: VPN_DEVICE_CREATED VPN_DEVICE_REVOKED VPN_DEVICE_CONFIG_ROTATED VPN_CONFIG_RENDERED VPN_DEVICE_SKIPPED_UNVERIFIED PENDING_TRIAL_ACCESS

phase-28-device-gates.gate-1: Pending email registration cannot create device rows, VPN clients, WireGuard peers, or downloadable config material.
phase-28-device-gates.gate-2: Verified activation creates exactly one primary device path or makes the existing primary-device creation explicitly idempotent.
phase-28-device-gates.gate-3: Verify token replay cannot create duplicate primary devices or duplicate peers.
phase-28-device-gates.gate-4: Authenticated device create, revoke, rotate, and config-render flows remain green after registration cutover.

phase-45-device-gates.gate-1: Verified users with pending trial state can create/reuse a primary device and retrieve first VPN config.
phase-45-device-gates.gate-2: Pending trial access does not bypass per-plan behavior for paid subscriptions or duplicate device replay safety.

scenarios.scenario-1: Primary device created after verified activation.
scenarios.scenario-2: Old peer disabled after revoke, new device works.
scenarios.scenario-3: Same logical device after rotate, old peer invalidated.
scenarios.scenario-4: Unverified registration does not provision VPN resources.

refresh-findings.finding-1: Full V-M-022 command failed locally with 404 responses in public VPN server/node compatibility route tests because those tests still used stale /api paths.
refresh-findings.finding-2: The 404 failures were stale test route expectations using /api instead of the canonical /api/v1 VPN prefixes. Public servers/nodes compatibility tests were aligned and the full V-M-022 command now passes locally.

V-M-022.evidence: 2026-05-13: Phase-28 backend suite passed locally and proved pending registration creates no user device while verified activation creates one primary device path and does not duplicate it on replay. 2026-05-19: Full V-M-022 command passed locally after aligning VPN compatibility route tests with /api/v1 prefixes. 2026-06-01: Phase-45 backend suite passed and confirms pending trial users remain eligible for first config retrieval.

phase-70-verification-design.scope: VPN device API and config compatibility verification for compact config page changes.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-1: /api/v1/vpn and device configuration routes must keep current response shape for config text, download, QR, and compatibility clients.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-2: Phase-70 frontend simplification must not remove device registration, active-device limits, or config availability checks.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-3: QR/download parity tests must cover at least one active device and one pending-trial/first-access compatible path.
phase-70-verification-design.commands: cd backend && pytest tests/test_device_api.py tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py tests/test_trial_activation.py; node scripts/phase70-config-qr-parity-smoke.mjs
